topic: Someone finds a wallet containing a large sum of cash
raw_output:
[story 1]

The worn leather of the wallet felt heavy in my palm, a surprising weight against my skin. It was thicker than usual, and when I finally managed to pry it open, the sight inside made my breath catch. Stacks of crisp bills, a significant amount of cash, spilled out onto the dusty floor of the bus stop bench. My heart began to hammer against my ribs, a frantic, uneven rhythm. I quickly checked the ID inside, my fingers trembling slightly as I scanned the name. It wasn't mine. A cold knot tightened in my stomach. I looked around, scanning the deserted street, suddenly hyper-aware of every shadow. How long had this been sitting here? A sudden urge to run, to disappear, washed over me.

[story 2]

I found the purse tucked beneath the newspaper on the park bench. When I opened it, the sheer volume of money inside made my hands shake uncontrollably. It was an astonishing amount; a fortune, perhaps. My gaze darted from the cash to the identification card, my eyes wide and unfocused for a moment. A clammy feeling started creeping up my neck. I tried to rationalize it—maybe someone had dropped it. But the silence of the afternoon seemed to amplify the sound of my own shallow breathing. I tucked the wallet close to my chest, my movements jerky and hesitant, every muscle tense as I considered what to do next.

[story 3]

It happened while I was cleaning out the lost and found box at the library. Tucked into an old jacket pocket was a wallet, and when I pulled it out, the sight of the bundled currency stopped me dead in my tracks. The paper felt brittle, and the money seemed impossibly bright under the fluorescent lights. A wave of intense unease washed over me, making my palms slick. I kept glancing toward the entrance, half expecting someone to materialize. I fumbled with the identification, my movements slow and deliberate, as if afraid to disturb the fragile arrangement. Every second stretched into an eternity as I processed the reality of what I was holding.
